title: Vercel confirms breach as hackers claim to be selling stolen data
summary: Cloud development platform Vercel has disclosed a security incident after threat actors claimed to have breached its systems and are attempting to sell stolen data. [...]

sections.overview: Cloud development platform Vercel has disclosed a security incident after threat actors claimed to have breached its systems and are attempting to sell stolen data. [...]
sections.technical: Cloud development platform Vercel has disclosed a security incident after threat actors claimed to have breached its systems and are attempting to sell stolen data. [...]
sections.impact:
Cloud development platform Vercel has disclosed a security incident after threat actors claimed to have breached its systems and are attempting to sell stolen data. [...]

Assessed severity: MEDIUM.
sections.mitigation: Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls. Affected users should rotate credentials, enable MFA, and monitor accounts for suspicious activity.
sections.context:
Timely vulnerability management and patch prioritization remain critical components of any defensive security program.

Data breaches carry significant regulatory, financial, and reputational consequences. Affected organizations and individuals should monitor for follow-on attacks using exposed data.

email_summary: Cloud development platform Vercel has disclosed a security incident after threat actors claimed to have breached its systems and are attempting to sell stolen data. [...].

title: Apple account change alerts abused to send phishing emails
summary: Apple account change notifications are being abused to send fake iPhone purchase phishing scams within legitimate emails sent from Apple's servers, increasing legitimacy and potentially allowing them to bypass spam filters. [...]

sections.overview: Apple account change notifications are being abused to send fake iPhone purchase phishing scams within legitimate emails sent from Apple's servers, increasing legitimacy and potentially allowing them to bypass spam filters. [...]
sections.technical: The attack leverages social engineering techniques to trick users into revealing credentials or executing malicious payloads.
sections.mitigation: Reinforce security awareness training. Ensure email filtering, link scanning, and multi-factor authentication are enabled.
sections.context: Phishing remains the most common initial access vector. Security awareness training combined with technical controls like email filtering and MFA are key defenses.

email_summary: Apple account change notifications are being abused to send fake iPhone purchase phishing scams within legitimate emails sent from Apple's servers, increasing legitimacy and potentially allowing them to bypass spam filters. [...].

title: NIST to stop rating non-priority flaws due to volume increase
summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology will stop assigning severity scores to lower-priority vulnerabilities due to the growing workload from rising submission volumes. [...]

sections.overview: The National Institute of Standards and Technology will stop assigning severity scores to lower-priority vulnerabilities due to the growing workload from rising submission volumes. [...]
sections.technical: This involves malicious software designed to compromise, exfiltrate, or disrupt targeted systems. A security vulnerability has been identified that could allow attackers to compromise affected systems.
sections.impact:
Assessed severity: MEDIUM.

Systems running affected software versions are at risk until patches or mitigations are applied.
sections.mitigation: Apply vendor-supplied patches as soon as possible. If patching is not immediately feasible, implement recommended workarounds and compensating controls. Review endpoint detection rules and update threat intelligence feeds. Scan for known indicators of compromise.
sections.context:
Timely vulnerability management and patch prioritization remain critical components of any defensive security program.

Modern malware campaigns frequently use multi-stage delivery chains and evasion techniques. Defense-in-depth strategies including EDR, network monitoring, and application allowlisting help reduce exposure.

email_summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology will stop assigning severity scores to lower-priority vulnerabilities due to the growing workload from rising submission volumes. [...].
